gap_summaries.title: Duplicate deps cause false-cycle detection / stuck scheduler
gap_summaries.details: A node whose deps list contained the same id twice (e.g. ['B1','B1']) accumulated in-degree of 2 but the topo loop decremented once per pop, so the node never reached zero in-degree. Result: false-positive cycle report at load time, and (had the policy somehow loaded) a stuck scheduler. Fixed by rejecting duplicate deps in _validate_closed_world with a clear error naming both the offending node and the duplicated dep.

gap_summaries.title: phase / version accepted bool because bool is a subclass of int
gap_summaries.details: isinstance(True, int) is True in Python, so a policy with 'phase': true or 'version': true passed type-validation and was coerced to phase=1/version=1 downstream. Fixed by adding an explicit bool guard before the int isinstance check for both the required() helper (when expected_type is int) and for the top-level version validator.
gap_summaries.fix: lifecycle_policy.py: bool short-circuit in required(); explicit bool guard on version. Tests: PolicyHardeningTests.test_phase_bool_rejected_despite_int_subclass, test_version_bool_rejected.

gap_summaries.title: load_status raised bare KeyError on missing required top-level fields
gap_summaries.details: Missing 'mode' / 'run_id' / 'started_at' / 'policy_hash' surfaced as KeyError, indistinguishable from a programmer error and not catchable by the (existing) ValueError contract documented for unknown NodeState values. Added explicit pre-check that raises ValueError naming the missing field. Same treatment applied to ArtifactRecord required fields.
gap_summaries.fix: lifecycle_status.py: required_field pre-loop in status_from_dict. Test: StatusSaveLoadRoundTripTests.test_status_missing_top_level_field_raises_value_error.

gap_summaries.title: Entry-mode router accepted nodes whose modes excluded the entry mode
gap_summaries.details: entry.greenfield could reference a node whose modes=['brownfield'] (and vice versa); the scheduler would then have no entry point in that mode and produce an empty runnable set silently. Added a closed-world check that the referenced node's modes list includes the entry mode key.
gap_summaries.fix: lifecycle_policy.py: mode-membership check in the entry-validation loop. Test: PolicyHardeningTests.test_entry_mode_mismatch_rejected.

gap_summaries.title: Unknown / typo'd node fields silently accepted
gap_summaries.details: A misspelled field like 'gat' instead of 'gate' was silently dropped (the loader only read known names); the node then carried the schema default for the real field and behaved unexpectedly at gate time. Added a closed-vocabulary check against a frozen set of allowed field names, raising PolicyError with the offending key(s) and the allowed list.
gap_summaries.fix: lifecycle_policy.py: _KNOWN_NODE_FIELDS frozenset + unknown-key check at the top of _parse_node. Test: PolicyHardeningTests.test_unknown_node_field_rejected.

gap_summaries.title: policy.version accepted zero / negative integers
gap_summaries.details: version was type-checked but not range-checked; 0, -1, or any int passed. Tightened to require version >= 1 so an obviously-broken doc fails up front rather than at consumer-version dispatch later.
gap_summaries.fix: lifecycle_policy.py: explicit < 1 check after the bool/int guard. Test: PolicyHardeningTests.test_version_zero_or_negative_rejected.

gap_summaries.title: save_status emitted insertion-ordered JSON
gap_summaries.details: json.dumps without sort_keys produced output whose top-level + nested key order depended on the order status_to_dict happened to populate the dicts. Two saves of an equivalent status could byte-differ, hurting audit-log diffs. Added sort_keys=True to align with the existing canonical_policy_json convention and asserted byte-equality of two successive saves.

gap_summaries.title: new_run_status accepted empty run_id / started_at
gap_summaries.details: Empty strings propagated into telemetry / file names / resume keys, where they break correlation but surface no clean error. Rejected at the constructor with a typed ValueError.
gap_summaries.fix: lifecycle_status.py: isinstance + non-empty guards in new_run_status. Tests: StatusSaveLoadRoundTripTests.test_new_run_status_rejects_empty_run_id, test_new_run_status_rejects_empty_started_at.

gap_summaries.title: Scheduler silently ignored / KeyError'd on policy-status drift
gap_summaries.details: When a node in the active policy set was absent from status.nodes (hand-edited or stale-against-policy status file), the scheduler either skipped via .get()-returns-None (for the node itself) or raised a bare KeyError (for a missing dep). Both modes hide a real bug. Replaced with explicit SchedulerError naming the offending node and the drift class.
gap_summaries.fix: lifecycle_scheduler.py: explicit None / missing-dep checks with SchedulerError. Test: ConcurrencyCapTests.test_policy_status_drift_raises_scheduler_error.

test_results.lifecycle_tests_after_fixes: 72 tests pass (11 new tests for the 9 gaps)
test_results.windows_native_suite: 1554 tests run; 6 failures + 4 errors are pre-existing Windows-portability issues in test_agent_config_model / test_runtime_policy / test_stop_hooks / test_state_policy_metadata (called out in the W0-M01 milestone changelog QA Notes as 'unrelated to W0-M01') — none touch the lifecycle modules.

scope_notes: Reviewed files = git diff --name-only 09a96885c77fe3ce53649132a4b8b1bd31e8fcdb HEAD = 3 implementation modules + 4 test files + 6 policy fixtures + 1 spec footnote + 1 changelog entry.

No changes were made to docs/changelog/260617.md or docs/superpowers/specs/lifecycle/build-spec-full.md — the M11 vocabulary-gate Gate-5 EXPECTED list already drifts vs the existing W0-M01 entry at line 32 (a pre-existing condition not in this Phase-C scope), and adding further dated headings would compound that drift.
All four lifecycle modules still well under the 500-LOC soft limit (policy 348, status 261, scheduler 144).
